meta.description: Cheese Ontology — canonical catalog populated (FOOD-04B). Intrinsic metadata only; relationships deferred to FOOD-04D/E.

cheese_category.description: Top-level cheese domain hub (single category in v1).

scientific_name_policy.purpose: Milk source species only — not cheese identity.
scientific_name_policy.mixed_milk_limitation: Schema v1 supports a single scientific_name per entity. Mixed-milk cheeses must use the primary milk source species in scientific_name and document secondary sources in external_ids.milk_sources (reserved) or editorial summary until a future schema extension.

cheese_category.description: Intrinsic classification aligned to taxonomy group.
cheese_category.extension_policy: New values require FOOD-04A governance amendment and catalog audit update.

milk_source.description: Primary milk source for pairing and dietary context.

texture.description: Primary eating texture at service temperature.

moisture_class.description: Moisture band for pairing weight and body reasoning.

origin_country.description: Canonical country of origin when geographically defined.
origin_country.allowed_values: ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 codes plus empty string for style-generic cheeses
origin_country.extension_policy: Use ISO codes only; regional subdivisions deferred to external_ids.

protected_status.extension_policy: New designation types require governance amendment.

milk_sources.format: array of milk_source vocabulary values for mixed-milk cheeses
milk_sources.note: Reserved extension for mixed-milk documentation without changing scientific_name semantics.

relationship_placeholders.description: Reserved arrays — must be present and empty [] until relationship mapping (FOOD-04D/E).

processed_cheese_policy.rationale: Processed cheese products are excluded unless a compelling pairing-relevant exception is approved during FOOD-04B review.

categories.name: Cheeses
categories.introduction: The Cheese Ontology catalogs canonical cheeses organized by rind style, texture, and aging class. Entities encode milk source, origin, and protection status for pairing reasoning.
categories.summary: Canonical cheese entities across nine taxonomy groups — fresh, bloomy rind, washed rind, natural rind, blue, semi-hard, hard, pasta filata, and brined.
categories.seo_description: Explore canonical cheeses by taxonomy group — milk source, aging, texture, rind type, and geographic protection status for wine pairing ontology.
